note: revenue growth decelerates to ~8%/yr as Embedded partner conversion stalls (K-3) and core POCUS faces GE Vscan Air / Philips Lumify / Clarius price competition (moat_map.md); multiple de-rates to 2.0x as the platform-optionality narrative fades and market re-prices BFLY as a struggling small-cap medtech; exit EV $349.7M vs current EV $1,871.4M -> deep permanent loss, not just underperformance.

revenue growth ~16%/yr (below FY26 guide's 20-24%, reflecting historical deceleration pattern 24.5%->19.0%->guided 20-24% and the reality that FY26 guide itself has not yet been proven over a full year); gross-margin trend (I1) continues to help unit economics but does not by itself re-rate the multiple; exit at 3.5x EV/revenue (still a premium multiple for a company that may or may not be sustainably profitable by FY31, reflecting continued -- if narrower -- cash burn and small-cap-medtech peer multiples); EVEN THE BASE CASE IS NEGATIVE at current entry price -- this is the central valuation finding.

revenue growth ~25%/yr (above FY26 guide's ~22% midpoint, requiring Butterfly Embedded to convert several of the '30-40 more in discussion' partners (E73) into signed, revenue-generating deals AND core POCUS to accelerate on Compass AI / Gestational Age AI adoption); exit at 5.0x EV/revenue (a full platform-company re-rate, roughly where BFLY trades on a FY26E guide basis TODAY -- i.e. bull case assumes the CURRENT rich multiple simply holds for 5 more years while growth compounds); STILL FALLS SHORT of the 8% hurdle at -2.0% IRR. This is the task's central honest finding: a standard/generous bull case is not sufficient -- the multiple would need to expand further (see aggressive-bull row), not just hold.

NON-BASE-CASE sensitivity row, shown for completeness only -- requires BOTH above-bull revenue growth (30%/yr, i.e. Butterfly Embedded becomes a much larger share of the business than any signed deal today supports) AND a multiple EXPANSION to 7.5x (i.e. the market must decide BFLY deserves a richer multiple than it has today, not just hold the current one) to clear the 8% hurdle. This combination (best-case growth stacked with further re-rating) is the ONLY row in this grid that clears 8%, which is the quantitative basis for the dossier's 'no margin of safety at 7.68' conclusion -- ordinary bull assumptions are not enough.
